perturbation.original_text: Service Provider shall perform the maintenance portion of the Services in a clean, safe, efficient and environmentally reasonable manner and maintain the SEF in good operating and mechanical condition in accordance with this Agreement and (i) all applicable laws and permits, (ii) all applicable express warranties and guarantees provided by manufacturers, suppliers, or Service Providers who provided materials or labor under the Engineering, Procurement and Construction Agreement dated September 30, 2009 (the "EPC Agreement") for the SEF subject to the terms and limitations thereof, and (iii) all manufacturer's maintenance instructions and specifications.
perturbation.changed_text: Service Provider shall perform the maintenance portion of the Services in a clean, safe, efficient and environmentally reasonable manner and maintain the SEF in good operating and mechanical condition in accordance with this Agreement and (i) all applicable laws and permits, (ii) all applicable express warranties and guarantees provided by manufacturers, suppliers, or Service Providers who provided materials or labor under the Engineering, Procurement and Construction Agreement dated September 30, 2009 (the "EPC Agreement") for the SEF subject to the terms and limitations thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(iii) all manufacturer's maintenance instructions and specifications' from Section 1.2, we create a scenario where the Service Provider is still obligated to maintain the SEF in good condition according to applicable laws and warranties, but the explicit requirement to adhere to manufacturer's instructions is omitted. This introduces a potential contradiction, because Section 1.3 requires the preservation of 'all warranties provided by manufacturers', which often depend on following the removed maintenance instructions. Omitting the explicit obligation to adhere to manufacturer’s instructions contradicts the overarching obligation to preserve manufacturer’s warranties, as compliance with manufacturer specifications is typically essential for maintaining warranty validity. Now service provider can claim it is not required to follow those instructions, as the text was omitted in Section 1.2, creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Service Provider shall perform the Services and its obligations under this Agreement in a manner that (a) insures the operation of the SEF within all required operational parameters and requirements, (b) preserves all warranties provided by manufacturers, suppliers, or Service Providers who provided materials or labor under the EPC Agreement relating to the SEF, subject to Force Majeure, (c) maintains the SEF, and 1 (d) seeks to minimize the variable operating costs of and wear and tear on the SEF, including using commercially reasonable efforts to achieve industry standard levels of SEF availability.
perturbation.justification: The omission of '(iii) all manufacturer's maintenance instructions and specifications' from the Changed Text (Section 1.2) creates a contradiction with the Contradicted Text (Section 1.3), which states that the Service Provider shall perform the Services in a manner that 'preserves all warranties provided by manufacturers'. Manufacturer warranties often depend on adherence to their maintenance instructions. By removing the explicit obligation to follow these instructions, the Changed Text creates a situation where the Service Provider can argue they are not required to follow manufacturer's instructions, thus potentially voiding the warranties, which contradicts the requirement to preserve those warranties. This represents a clear and impactful in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Services shall not include, and Service Provider shall not be responsible for, any operations, maintenance, repair, or other services beyond the Services set forth in Exhibit C (such non-covered services referred to hereinafter as "Non-Covered Services"). All work associated with Non- Covered Services will be billed according to the terms of Section 2.2. Any studies or other services required by Owner to review options to optimize system performance will be provided as Non-Covered Services.
perturbation.changed_text: The Services shall not include, and Service Provider shall not be responsible for, any operations, maintenance, repair, or other services beyond the Services set forth in Exhibit C (such non-covered services referred to hereinafter as "Non-Covered Services"). All work associated with Non- Covered Services will be billed according to the terms of Section 2.2.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the sentence 'Any studies or other services required by Owner to review options to optimize system performance will be provided as Non-Covered Services.' from Section 1.4, we introduce ambiguity. While the Service Provider is not responsible for services beyond Exhibit C, the specific exclusion of system optimization studies is removed. This creates an internal contradiction because, without this clarification, the Owner could argue that system optimization studies *are* part of the general 'Services' defined in Exhibit C, especially if Exhibit C is vaguely worded. This increases the likelihood of disputes and uncertainty in enforcement, particularly if there is pressure from the Owner for Service Provider to do this without additional compensation. The services were omitted and are no longer clarified as Non-Covered, which creates uncertainty and contradictions withing the contract. The owner can pressure the provider now.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Services shall not include, and Service Provider shall not be responsible for, any operations, maintenance, repair, or other services beyond the Services set forth in Exhibit C (such non-covered services referred to hereinafter as "Non-Covered Services"). All work associated with Non- Covered Services will be billed according to the terms of Section 2.2.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the sentence 'Any studies or other services required by Owner to review options to optimize system performance will be provided as Non-Covered Services' introduces ambiguity regarding whether system optimization studies are covered under the general 'Services' defined in Exhibit C. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which states that the Service Provider is not responsible for services beyond Exhibit C. The ambiguity introduced by the omission makes it unclear whether system optimization studies are included, potentially contradicting the explicitly stated limitation of responsibility. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.
